1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)

42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""

7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

The `Bookmark` class is a dataclass with the following fields:

```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str                    # UUID string identifier
    url: str                   # URL of the bookmarked resource
    title: str                 # Display title for the bookmark
    tags: List[str]            # List of tag strings (default: empty list)
    notes: str                 # Optional notes/description (default: empty string)
    created_at: str            # ISO format timestamp (auto-generated on creation)
```

**Key Methods:**
- `to_dict()` → `dict`: Serializes bookmark to dictionary format
- `from_dict(data: dict) → Bookmark` (classmethod): Deserializes from dictionary

### 3. Bookmark Storage and Loading

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775382892790-p3vps3/src/storage.py`

**Storage Format:** JSON file at `~/.bookmarks.json` (default location, configurable via `db_path` parameter)

**Functions:**

1. **`load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) → List[Bookmark]`**
   - Returns empty list if file doesn't exist
   - Deserializes JSON array into Bookmark objects

2. **`save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) → None`**
   - Serializes Bookmark list to JSON file with 2-space indentation
   - Creates file if it doesn't exist

3. **`add_bookmark(url: str, title: str, tags: Optional[List[str]] = None, notes: str = "", db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) → Bookmark`**
   - Creates new Bookmark with UUID-generated ID
   - Auto-generates timestamp in ISO format
   - Persists to storage immediately
   - Returns the created Bookmark object

4. **`delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) → bool`**
   - Returns True if bookmark found and deleted, False otherwise
   - Persists deletion immediately

5. **`get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) → Optional[Bookmark]`**
   - Retrieves single bookmark by ID
   - Returns None if not found

### 4. Filtering/Querying Utility Functions

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775382892790-p3vps3/tests/test_search.py` (defines expected behavior)

The test file expects the following search functions to be implemented in a new `src/search.py` module (currently does not exist):

1. **`search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) → List[Bookmark]`**
   - Case-insensitive substring matching on title
   - Returns all bookmarks with matching titles

2. **`search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) → List[Bookmark]`**
   - Exact tag matching (case-sensitive)
   - Returns all bookmarks with the specified tag

3. **`search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) → List[Bookmark]`**
   - Extracts domain from URL and matches against provided domain
   - Supports subdomain matching

4. **`search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: str = None, tag: str = None, domain: str = None) → List[Bookmark]`**
   - Combined search with AND logic when multiple criteria provided
   - Returns all bookmarks if no criteria provided
   - Returns empty list if criteria conflict (no matches)

### 5. CLI Structure

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775382892790-p3vps3/src/cli.py`

**Entry Point:** `bookmark` command (from `src.cli:main`)

**Implemented Commands:**

1. **`bookmark add <url> <title>`**
   - Options: `--tags TAG1 TAG2 ...`, `--notes "notes text"`
   - Creates and persists new bookmark

2. **`bookmark list`**
   - Options: `--format {table|json}` (default: table)
   - Lists all bookmarks

3. **`bookmark delete <id>`**
   - Deletes bookmark by ID
   - Exits with code 1 if not found

   - Displays single bookmark
   - Exits with code 1 if not found

   - Options: `--title QUERY`, `--tag TAG`, `--domain DOMAIN`, `--format {table|json}` (default: table)
   - Searches bookmarks using combined criteria

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775382892790-p3vps3/src/formatter.py`

**Functions:**

1. **`format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) → str`**
   - Renders bookmarks as fixed-width ASCII table
   - Column widths: ID (8), Title (30), URL (40), Tags (20)
   - Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty list

The data model uses Python dataclasses for type safety, JSON for persistence, and follows standard CLI patterns using argparse. import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),

            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")

# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI tool (`bookmark-cli`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to search/filter bookmarks. The task is to add search functionality via a new `src/search.py` module and wire it into the CLI as a `search` command. A comprehensive test file (`tests/test_search.py`) already exists and defines the exact expected behavior.

## Files to Create
- **`src/search.py`** — New module with search functions

## Files to Modify
- **`src/cli.py`** — Add `search` subcommand to the parser and dispatch logic

## Files to Verify (read-only)
- `tests/test_search.py` — Pre-existing tests that define expected behavior
- `tests/test_storage.py` — Existing tests that must continue to pass
- `src/models.py` — Bookmark dataclass (fields: `id`, `url`, `title`, `tags`, `notes`, `created_at`)
- `src/formatter.py` — Reuse `format_table()` and `format_json()` for output

---

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create the file with these four functions:

### `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Convert both `b.title` and `query` to lowercase, then check if `query` is a substring of `b.title`.
- Return list of matching bookmarks.

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Check if `tag` is in `b.tags` (exact string match within the tags list).
- Return list of matching bookmarks.

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Use `urllib.parse.urlparse(b.url).hostname` to extract the domain from each bookmark's URL.
- Compare extracted hostname exactly to the provided `domain` string.
- Return list of matching bookmarks.
- **Note:** `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html").hostname` returns `"docs.python.org"`, which is what the tests expect.

### `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Start with the full `bookmarks` list as candidates.
- If `title` is not None, filter candidates through `search_by_title`.
- If `tag` is not None, filter candidates through `search_by_tag`.
- If `domain` is not None, filter candidates through `search_by_domain`.
- AND logic is achieved by chaining filters sequentially on the remaining candidates.
- If no criteria provided, return all bookmarks.

**Imports needed:** `urllib.parse`, `List` from `typing`, `Bookmark` from `.models`.

---

## Step 2: Add `search` subcommand to `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Update imports (line 4)
Add import of the `search` function from `.search`:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add search subparser in `build_parser()` (after the `show` parser block, ~line 39)
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact match)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Add search command handler in `main()` (after the `show` elif block, ~line 81)
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

This reuses the existing `format_table` and `format_json` formatters and `load_bookmarks` from storage — no new dependencies.

---

## Verification

Run all tests to confirm:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected results:
- All tests in `tests/test_storage.py` pass (unchanged code)
- All tests in `tests/test_search.py` pass (new functionality)
  - `TestSearchByTitle` (3 tests): case-insensitive, substring, no-match
  - `TestSearchByTag` (3 tests): exact, shared, no-match
  - `TestSearchByDomain` (3 tests): domain, subdomain, no-match
  - `TestSearchCombined` (4 tests): single criteria, AND logic, no-criteria, conflicting
  - `TestSearchCLI` (2 tests): parser accepts `search` command, `--format json` flag

"""Search and filter functionality for bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains *query* (case-insensitive)."""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for *tag*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches *domain*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).hostname == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks by combining criteria with AND logic.

    Any subset of *title*, *tag*, and *domain* may be provided.  When
    multiple criteria are given the result set is the intersection of
    each individual filter.  If no criteria are provided every bookmark
    is returned.
    """
    results = list(bookmarks)
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
